verses.sanskrit: श्रीऋषिरुवाचएवं जिहासुर्नृप देहमाजौमृत्युं वरं विजयान्मन्यमान: ।शूलं प्रगृह्याभ्यपतत् सुरेन्द्रंयथा महापुरुषं कैटभोऽप्सु ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-ṛṣir uvācaevaṁ jihāsur nṛpa deham ājaumṛtyuṁ varaṁ vijayān manyamānaḥśūlaṁ pragṛhyābhyapatat surendraṁyathā mahā-puruṣaṁ kaiṭabho ’psu
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Desiring to give up his body, Vṛtrāsura considered death in the battle preferable to victory. O King Parīkṣit, he vigorously took up his trident and with great force attacked Lord Indra, the King of heaven, just as Kaiṭabha had forcefully attacked the Supreme Personality of Godhead when the universe was inundated.

verses.sanskrit: ततो युगान्ताग्निकठोरजिह्व-माविध्य शूलं तरसासुरेन्द्र: ।क्षिप्त्वा महेन्द्राय विनद्य वीरोहतोऽसि पापेति रुषा जगाद ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato yugāntāgni-kaṭhora-jihvamāvidhya śūlaṁ tarasāsurendraḥkṣiptvā mahendrāya vinadya vīrohato ’si pāpeti ruṣā jagāda
verses.text: Then Vṛtrāsura, the great hero of the demons, whirled his trident, which had points like the flames of the blazing fire at the end of the millennium. With great force and anger he threw it at Indra, roaring and exclaiming loudly, “O sinful one, thus shall I kill you!”

verses.sanskrit: ख आपतत्तद्विचलद्ग्रहोल्कव-न्निरीक्ष्य दुष्प्रेक्ष्यमजातविक्लव: ।वज्रेण वज्री शतपर्वणाच्छिनद्भुजं च तस्योरगराजभोगम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kha āpatat tad vicalad graholkavannirīkṣya duṣprekṣyam ajāta-viklavaḥvajreṇa vajrī śata-parvaṇācchinadbhujaṁ ca tasyoraga-rāja-bhogam
verses.text: Flying in the sky, Vṛtrāsura’s trident resembled a brilliant meteor. Although the blazing weapon was difficult to look upon, King Indra, unafraid, cut it to pieces with his thunderbolt. Simultaneously, he cut off one of Vṛtrāsura’s arms, which was as thick as the body of Vāsuki, the King of the serpents.

verses.sanskrit: छिन्नैकबाहु: परिघेण वृत्र:संरब्ध आसाद्य गृहीतवज्रम् ।हनौ तताडेन्द्रमथामरेभंवज्रं च हस्तान्न्यपतन्मघोन: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: chinnaika-bāhuḥ parigheṇa vṛtraḥsaṁrabdha āsādya gṛhīta-vajramhanau tatāḍendram athāmarebhaṁvajraṁ ca hastān nyapatan maghonaḥ
verses.text: Although one of his arms was severed from his body, Vṛtrāsura angrily approached King Indra and struck him on the jaw with an iron mace. He also struck the elephant that carried Indra. Thus Indra dropped the thunderbolt from his hand.

verses.sanskrit: वृत्रस्य कर्मातिमहाद्भ‍ुतं तत्सुरासुराश्चारणसिद्धसङ्घा: ।अपूजयंस्तत् पुरुहूतसङ्कटंनिरीक्ष्य हा हेति विचुक्रुशुर्भृशम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: vṛtrasya karmāti-mahādbhutaṁ tatsurāsurāś cāraṇa-siddha-saṅghāḥapūjayaṁs tat puruhūta-saṅkaṭaṁnirīkṣya hā heti vicukruśur bhṛśam
verses.text: The denizens of various planets, like the demigods, demons, Cāraṇas and Siddhas, praised Vṛtrāsura’s deed, but when they observed that Indra was in great danger, they lamented, “Alas! Alas!”

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रो न वज्रं जगृहे विलज्जित-श्‍च्युतं स्वहस्तादरिसन्निधौ पुन: ।तमाह वृत्रो हर आत्तवज्रोजहि स्वशत्रुं न विषादकाल: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: indro na vajraṁ jagṛhe vilajjitaścyutaṁ sva-hastād ari-sannidhau punaḥtam āha vṛtro hara ātta-vajrojahi sva-śatruṁ na viṣāda-kālaḥ
verses.text: Having dropped the thunderbolt from his hand in the presence of his enemy, Indra was practically defeated and was very much ashamed. He dared not pick up his weapon again. Vṛtrāsura, however, encouraged him, saying, “Take up your thunderbolt and kill your enemy. This is not the time to lament your fate.”

verses.sanskrit: युयुत्सतां कुत्रचिदाततायिनांजय: सदैकत्र न वै परात्मनाम् ।विनैकमुत्पत्तिलयस्थितीश्वरंसर्वज्ञमाद्यं पुरुषं सनातनम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: yuyutsatāṁ kutracid ātatāyināṁjayaḥ sadaikatra na vai parātmanāmvinaikam utpatti-laya-sthitīśvaraṁsarvajñam ādyaṁ puruṣaṁ sanātanam
verses.text: Vṛtrāsura continued: O Indra, no one is guaranteed of being always victorious but the original enjoyer, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Bhagavān. He is the cause of creation, maintenance and annihilation, and He knows everything. Being dependent and being obliged to accept material bodies, belligerent subordinates are sometimes victorious and sometimes defeated.

verses.sanskrit: लोका: सपाला यस्येमे श्वसन्ति विवशा वशे ।द्विजा इव शिचा बद्धा: स काल इह कारणम् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: lokāḥ sapālā yasyemeśvasanti vivaśā vaśedvijā iva śicā baddhāḥsa kāla iha kāraṇam
verses.text: All living beings in all the planets of this universe, including the presiding deities of all the planets, are fully under the control of the Lord. They work like birds caught in a net, who cannot move independently.

verses.sanskrit: ओज: सहो बलं प्राणममृतं मृत्युमेव च ।तमज्ञाय जनो हेतुमात्मानं मन्यते जडम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: ojaḥ saho balaṁ prāṇamamṛtaṁ mṛtyum eva catam ajñāya jano hetumātmānaṁ manyate jaḍam
verses.text: Our sensory prowess, mental power, bodily strength, living force, immortality and mortality are all subject to the superintendence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Not knowing this, foolish people think the dull material body to be the cause of their activities.

verses.sanskrit: यथा दारुमयी नारी यथा पत्रमयो मृग: ।एवं भूतानि मघवन्नीशतन्त्राणि विद्धि भो: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā dārumayī nārīyathā patramayo mṛgaḥevaṁ bhūtāni maghavannīśa-tantrāṇi viddhi bhoḥ
verses.text: O King Indra, as a wooden doll that looks like a woman or as an animal made of grass and leaves cannot move or dance independently, but depends fully on the person who handles it, all of us dance according to the desire of the supreme controller, the Personality of Godhead. No one is independent.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुष: प्रकृतिर्व्यक्तमात्मा भूतेन्द्रियाशया: ।शक्नुवन्त्यस्य सर्गादौ न विना यदनुग्रहात् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: puruṣaḥ prakṛtir vyaktamātmā bhūtendriyāśayāḥśaknuvanty asya sargādauna vinā yad-anugrahāt
verses.text: The three puruṣas — Kāraṇodakaśāyī Viṣṇu, Garbhodakaśāyī Viṣṇu and Kṣīrodakaśāyī-Viṣṇu — the material nature, the total material energy, the false ego, the five material elements, the material senses, the mind, the intelligence and consciousness cannot create the material manifestation without the direction of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: अविद्वानेवमात्मानं मन्यतेऽनीशमीश्वरम् ।भूतै: सृजति भूतानि ग्रसते तानि तै: स्वयम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: avidvān evam ātmānaṁmanyate ’nīśam īśvarambhūtaiḥ sṛjati bhūtānigrasate tāni taiḥ svayam
verses.text: A foolish, senseless person cannot understand the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Although always dependent, he falsely thinks himself the Supreme. If one thinks, “According to one’s previous fruitive actions, one’s material body is created by the father and mother, and the same body is annihilated by another agent, as another animal is devoured by a tiger,” this is not proper understanding. The Supreme Personality of Godhead Himself creates and devours the living beings through other living beings.

verses.sanskrit: आयु: श्री: कीर्तिरैश्वर्यमाशिष: पुरुषस्य या: ।भवन्त्येव हि तत्काले यथानिच्छोर्विपर्यया: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: āyuḥ śrīḥ kīrtir aiśvaryamāśiṣaḥ puruṣasya yāḥbhavanty eva hi tat-kāleyathānicchor viparyayāḥ
verses.text: Just as a person not inclined to die must nonetheless give up his longevity, opulence, fame and everything else at the time of death, so, at the appointed time of victory, one can gain all these when the Supreme Lord awards them by His mercy.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मादकीर्तियशसोर्जयापजययोरपि ।सम: स्यात्सुखदु:खाभ्यां मृत्युजीवितयोस्तथा ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmād akīrti-yaśasorjayāpajayayor apisamaḥ syāt sukha-duḥkhābhyāṁmṛtyu-jīvitayos tathā
verses.text: Since everything is dependent on the supreme will of the Personality of Godhead, one should be equipoised in fame and defamation, victory and defeat, life and death. In their effects, represented as happiness and distress, one should maintain oneself in equilibrium, without anxiety.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति प्रकृतेर्नात्मनो गुणा: ।तत्र साक्षिणमात्मानं यो वेद स न बध्यते ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sattvaṁ rajas tama itiprakṛter nātmano guṇāḥtatra sākṣiṇam ātmānaṁyo veda sa na badhyate
verses.text: One who knows that the three qualities — goodness, passion and ignorance — are not qualities of the soul but qualities of material nature, and who knows that the pure soul is simply an observer of the actions and reactions of these qualities, should be understood to be a liberated person. He is not bound by these qualities.

verses.sanskrit: पश्य मां निर्जितं शत्रु वृक्णायुधभुजं मृधे ।घटमानं यथाशक्ति तव प्राणजिहीर्षया ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: paśya māṁ nirjitaṁ śatruvṛkṇāyudha-bhujaṁ mṛdheghaṭamānaṁ yathā-śaktitava prāṇa-jihīrṣayā
verses.text: O my enemy, just look at me. I have already been defeated, for my weapon and arm have been cut to pieces. You have already overwhelmed me, but nonetheless, with a desire to kill you, I am trying my best to fight. I am not at all morose, even under such adverse conditions. Therefore you should give up your moroseness and continue fighting.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणग्लहोऽयं समर इष्वक्षो वाहनासन: ।अत्र न ज्ञायतेऽमुष्य जयोऽमुष्य पराजय: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāṇa-glaho ’yaṁ samaraiṣv-akṣo vāhanāsanaḥatra na jñāyate ’muṣyajayo ’muṣya parājayaḥ
verses.text: O my enemy, consider this battle a gambling match in which our lives are the stakes, the arrows are the dice, and the animals acting as carriers are the game board. No one can understand who will be defeated and who will be victorious. It all depends on providence.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइन्द्रो वृत्रवच: श्रुत्वा गतालीकमपूजयत् ।गृहीतवज्र: प्रहसंस्तमाह गतविस्मय: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaindro vṛtra-vacaḥ śrutvāgatālīkam apūjayatgṛhīta-vajraḥ prahasaṁstam āha gata-vismayaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Hearing the straightforward, instructive words of Vṛtrāsura, King Indra praised him and again took the thunderbolt in his hand. Without bewilderment or duplicity, he then smiled and spoke to Vṛtrāsura as follows.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्र उवाचअहो दानव सिद्धोऽसि यस्य ते मतिरीद‍ृशी ।भक्त: सर्वात्मनात्मानं सुहृदं जगदीश्वरम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: indra uvācaaho dānava siddho ’siyasya te matir īdṛśībhaktaḥ sarvātmanātmānaṁsuhṛdaṁ jagad-īśvaram
verses.text: Indra said: O great demon, I see by your discrimination and endurance in devotional service, despite your dangerous position, that you are a perfect devotee of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the Supersoul and friend of everyone.

verses.sanskrit: भवानतार्षीन्मायां वै वैष्णवीं जनमोहिनीम् ।यद् विहायासुरं भावं महापुरुषतां गत: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: bhavān atārṣīn māyāṁ vaivaiṣṇavīṁ jana-mohinīmyad vihāyāsuraṁ bhāvaṁmahā-puruṣatāṁ gataḥ
verses.text: You have surmounted the illusory energy of Lord Viṣṇu, and because of this liberation, you have given up the demoniac mentality and have attained the position of an exalted devotee.

verses.sanskrit: खल्विदं महदाश्चर्यं यद् रज:प्रकृतेस्तव ।वासुदेवे भगवति सत्त्वात्मनि दृढा मति: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: khalv idaṁ mahad āścaryaṁyad rajaḥ-prakṛtes tavavāsudeve bhagavatisattvātmani dṛḍhā matiḥ
verses.text: O Vṛtrāsura, demons are generally conducted by the mode of passion. Therefore, what a great wonder it is that although you are a demon, you have adopted the mentality of a devotee and have fixed your mind on the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Vāsudeva, who is always situated in pure goodness.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य भक्तिर्भगवति हरौ नि:श्रेयसेश्वरे ।विक्रीडतोऽमृताम्भोधौ किं क्षुद्रै: खातकोदकै: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yasya bhaktir bhagavatiharau niḥśreyaseśvarevikrīḍato ’mṛtāmbhodhaukiṁ kṣudraiḥ khātakodakaiḥ
verses.text: A person fixed in the devotional service of the Supreme Lord, Hari, the Lord of the highest auspiciousness, swims in the ocean of nectar. For him what is the use of the water in small ditches?

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइति ब्रुवाणावन्योन्यं धर्मजिज्ञासया नृप ।युयुधाते महावीर्याविन्द्रवृत्रौ युधाम्पती ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaiti bruvāṇāv anyonyaṁdharma-jijñāsayā nṛpayuyudhāte mahā-vīryāvindra-vṛtrau yudhām patī
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Vṛtrāsura and King Indra spoke about devotional service even on the battlefield, and then as a matter of duty they again began fighting. My dear King, both of them were great fighters and were equally powerful.

verses.sanskrit: आविध्य परिघं वृत्र: कार्ष्णायसमरिन्दम: ।इन्द्राय प्राहिणोद् घोरं वामहस्तेन मारिष ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: āvidhya parighaṁ vṛtraḥkārṣṇāyasam arindamaḥindrāya prāhiṇod ghoraṁvāma-hastena māriṣa
verses.text: O Mahārāja Parīkṣit, Vṛtrāsura, who was completely able to subdue his enemy, took his iron club, whirled it around, aimed it at Indra and then threw it at him with his left hand.

verses.sanskrit: स तु वृत्रस्य परिघं करं च करभोपमम् ।चिच्छेद युगपद्देवो वज्रेण शतपर्वणा ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa tu vṛtrasya parighaṁkaraṁ ca karabhopamamciccheda yugapad devovajreṇa śata-parvaṇā
verses.text: With his thunderbolt named Śataparvan, Indra simultaneously cut to pieces Vṛtrāsura’s club and his remaining hand.

verses.sanskrit: दोर्भ्यामुत्कृत्तमूलाभ्यां बभौ रक्तस्रवोऽसुर: ।छिन्नपक्षो यथा गोत्र: खाद्भ्रष्टो वज्रिणा हत: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: dorbhyām utkṛtta-mūlābhyāṁbabhau rakta-sravo ’suraḥchinna-pakṣo yathā gotraḥkhād bhraṣṭo vajriṇā hataḥ
verses.text: Vṛtrāsura, bleeding profusely, his two arms cut off at their roots, looked very beautiful, like a flying mountain whose wings have been cut to pieces by Indra.

verses.sanskrit: महाप्राणो महावीर्यो महासर्प इव द्विपम् ।कृत्वाधरां हनुं भूमौ दैत्यो दिव्युत्तरां हनुम् ।नभोगम्भीरवक्त्रेण लेलिहोल्बणजिह्वया ॥ २७ ॥दंष्ट्राभि: कालकल्पाभिर्ग्रसन्निव जगत्‍त्रयम् ।अतिमात्रमहाकाय आक्षिपंस्तरसा गिरीन् ॥ २८ ॥गिरिराट् पादचारीव पद्भ्यां निर्जरयन् महीम् ।जग्रास स समासाद्य वज्रिणं सहवाहनम् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: mahā-prāṇo mahā-vīryomahā-sarpa iva dvipamkṛtvādharāṁ hanuṁ bhūmaudaityo divy uttarāṁ hanum
verses.text: Vṛtrāsura was very powerful in physical strength and influence. He placed his lower jaw on the ground and his upper jaw in the sky. His mouth became very deep, like the sky itself, and his tongue resembled a large serpent. With his fearful, deathlike teeth, he seemed to be trying to devour the entire universe. Thus assuming a gigantic body, the great demon Vṛtrāsura shook even the mountains and began crushing the surface of the earth with his legs, as if he were the Himālayas walking about. He came before Indra and swallowed him and Airāvata, his carrier, just as a big python might swallow an elephant.

verses.sanskrit: वृत्रग्रस्तं तमालोक्य सप्रजापतय: सुरा: ।हा कष्टमिति निर्विण्णाश्चुक्रुशु: समहर्षय: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: vṛtra-grastaṁ tam ālokyasaprajāpatayaḥ surāḥhā kaṣṭam iti nirviṇṇāścukruśuḥ samaharṣayaḥ
verses.text: When the demigods, along with Brahmā, other prajāpatis and other great saintly persons, saw that Indra had been swallowed by the demon, they became very morose. “Alas,” they lamented. “What a calamity! What a calamity!”

verses.sanskrit: निगीर्णोऽप्यसुरेन्द्रेण न ममारोदरं गत: ।महापुरुषसन्नद्धो योगमायाबलेन च ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: nigīrṇo ’py asurendreṇana mamārodaraṁ gataḥmahāpuruṣa-sannaddhoyoga-māyā-balena ca
verses.text: The protective armor of Nārāyaṇa, which Indra possessed, was identical with Nārāyaṇa Himself, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Protected by that armor and by his own mystic power, King Indra, although swallowed by Vṛtrāsura, did not die within the demon’s belly.

verses.sanskrit: भित्त्वा वज्रेण तत्कुक्षिं निष्क्रम्य बलभिद् विभु: ।उच्चकर्त शिर: शत्रोर्गिरिश‍ृङ्गमिवौजसा ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhittvā vajreṇa tat-kukṣiṁniṣkramya bala-bhid vibhuḥuccakarta śiraḥ śatrorgiri-śṛṅgam ivaujasā
verses.text: With his thunderbolt, King Indra, who was also extremely powerful, pierced through Vṛtrāsura’s abdomen and came out. Indra, the killer of the demon Bala, then immediately cut off Vṛtrāsura’s head, which was as high as the peak of a mountain.

verses.sanskrit: वज्रस्तु तत्कन्धरमाशुवेग:कृन्तन् समन्तात् परिवर्तमान: ।न्यपातयत् तावदहर्गणेनयो ज्योतिषामयने वार्त्रहत्ये ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: vajras tu tat-kandharam āśu-vegaḥkṛntan samantāt parivartamānaḥnyapātayat tāvad ahar-gaṇenayo jyotiṣām ayane vārtra-hatye
verses.text: Although the thunderbolt revolved around Vṛtrāsura’s neck with great speed, separating his head from his body took one complete year — 360 days, the time in which the sun, moon and other luminaries complete a northern and southern journey. Then, at the suitable time for Vṛtrāsura to be killed, his head fell to the ground.

verses.sanskrit: तदा च खे दुन्दुभयो विनेदु-र्गन्धर्वसिद्धा: समहर्षिसङ्घा: ।वार्त्रघ्नलिङ्गैस्तमभिष्टुवानामन्त्रैर्मुदा कुसुमैरभ्यवर्षन् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tadā ca khe dundubhayo vinedurgandharva-siddhāḥ samaharṣi-saṅghāḥvārtra-ghna-liṅgais tam abhiṣṭuvānāmantrair mudā kusumair abhyavarṣan
verses.text: When Vṛtrāsura was killed, the Gandharvas and Siddhas in the heavenly planets beat kettledrums in jubilation. With Vedic hymns they celebrated the prowess of Indra, the killer of Vṛtrāsura, praising Indra and showering flowers upon him with great pleasure.

verses.sanskrit: वृत्रस्य देहान्निष्क्रान्तमात्मज्योतिररिन्दम ।पश्यतां सर्वदेवानामलोकं समपद्यत ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: vṛtrasya dehān niṣkrāntamātma-jyotir arindamapaśyatāṁ sarva-devānāmalokaṁ samapadyata
verses.text: O King Parīkṣit, subduer of enemies, the living spark then came forth from Vṛtrāsura’s body and returned home, back to Godhead. While all the demigods looked on, he entered the transcendental world to become an associate of Lord Saṅkarṣaṇa.
